AreaTypes.brief: Constants defining various area types used for categorizing regions within the game system.
AreaTypes.details: The AreaTypes group provides a collection of character constants that represent different classifications of areas in the game environment. These include general categories such as 'All' and 'Normal', as well as specific types like 'Clan', 'IMMS', 'Hero', 'Arena', and a special 'XXX' type for custom or undefined areas. These constants are used throughout the codebase to identify, filter, and manage areas consistently, supporting features such as access permissions, gameplay mechanics, display logic, and organizational structure within the game. By standardizing area type identifiers, the system ensures maintainability and clarity when handling various region-specific behaviors and attributes.
AreaTypes.notes: The constants are designed to be used as character identifiers for quick and efficient comparisons. They are organized to cover both common and specialized area types, facilitating flexible categorization and future extension.
AreaTypes.rationale: This group exists to provide a standardized set of identifiers for different area categories, enabling consistent handling and interpretation of area-related data across the system. It supports modularity and maintainability by centralizing area type definitions, which are crucial for implementing game mechanics, access control, and user interface features related to different regions.
AreaTypes.name: AreaTypes

AREA_TYPE_ALL.brief: A constant representing the 'All' area type within the system.
AREA_TYPE_ALL.details: The `AREA_TYPE_ALL` constant is used to denote an area that includes all possible categories or types in the application. It is initialized with the character 'A' and serves as a standard identifier for the inclusive or comprehensive area type. This constant is utilized in various parts of the codebase, such as switch-case statements for display labels and default configurations when the level range is set to 'ALL'. Its consistent use ensures clarity and uniformity when referencing the all-encompassing area type across different modules.
AREA_TYPE_ALL.notes: The constant is stored as a single character and is often used in conditional logic and display functions to represent the universal area category.
AREA_TYPE_ALL.rationale: This constant exists to provide a clear, standardized way to refer to the 'all' area type, facilitating consistent handling and representation of comprehensive areas within the system.
AREA_TYPE_ALL.name: AREA_TYPE_ALL

usages.area__handler_8cc, void do_areas(Charact: Used in switch-case to determine the display label for areas of type ALL.
usages.class_area, int Area::scan_credits(): Used to set the default area_type when the level range string is 'ALL'.

AREA_TYPE_CLAN.brief: A constant character representing the 'Clan' type of area within the game.
AREA_TYPE_CLAN.details: This constant, initialized with the character 'C', is used to identify areas associated with clans in the game. It is referenced in various functions, such as area scanning and display routines, to categorize and process clan-related areas consistently. Its usage ensures that all parts of the codebase recognize and handle clan areas uniformly, facilitating features like level range setting and area labeling.
AREA_TYPE_CLAN.notes: The constant is stored as a character literal and is used as a key identifier in switch statements and conditionals to distinguish clan areas from other types.
AREA_TYPE_CLAN.rationale: The constant exists to provide a clear, maintainable way to identify clan areas throughout the code, supporting consistent behavior and simplifying updates related to area type handling.
AREA_TYPE_CLAN.name: AREA_TYPE_CLAN

AREA_TYPE_IMMS.brief: Constant representing the 'IMMS' area type identifier used for categorizing specific area levels.
AREA_TYPE_IMMS.details: The `AREA_TYPE_IMMS` constant is a character literal 'I' that uniquely identifies areas classified as 'IMMS' within the system. It is primarily used during parsing, assignment, and display of area information related to 'IMMS' level ranges. Functions such as `Area::scan_credits()` utilize this constant to set the `area_type` when the level range string matches 'IMM' or 'IMMS', ensuring consistent categorization. Additionally, display routines reference this constant to label 'IMMS' areas appropriately, facilitating clear and uniform identification across the codebase.
AREA_TYPE_IMMS.notes: The constant is stored as a character literal to optimize comparisons and storage. Its usage pattern emphasizes consistent referencing for 'IMMS' area types in parsing and display functions.
AREA_TYPE_IMMS.rationale: This constant exists to provide a clear, standardized identifier for 'IMMS' areas, enabling reliable categorization and display logic within the system, and supporting functions that depend on area type distinctions.
AREA_TYPE_IMMS.name: AREA_TYPE_IMMS

AREA_TYPE_HERO.brief: A constant character representing the 'Hero' area type in the game.
AREA_TYPE_HERO.details: The constant `AREA_TYPE_HERO` is defined as the character literal 'H' and is used to identify areas classified as 'Hero' type within the game environment. It is utilized in functions such as `Area::scan_credits()` to set the area type based on level range strings like 'HERO' or 'HRO', and in display functions to determine the appropriate label for hero-type areas. By standardizing this value, the constant ensures consistent recognition and handling of hero areas throughout the codebase.
AREA_TYPE_HERO.notes: This constant is stored as a single character to facilitate efficient comparisons and parsing operations. Its usage pattern aligns with other area type constants, promoting uniformity in area classification logic.
AREA_TYPE_HERO.rationale: The constant exists to provide a clear, maintainable way to identify hero-type areas, supporting game mechanics that depend on differentiating these areas for display, level restrictions, or gameplay logic.
AREA_TYPE_HERO.name: AREA_TYPE_HERO

AREA_TYPE_ARENA.brief: A constant character representing the 'Arena' area type identifier.
AREA_TYPE_ARENA.details: The `AREA_TYPE_ARENA` constant is defined as the character literal 'R' and serves as a unique identifier for areas classified as arenas within the system. It is used in various functions to set, check, or display the type of an area, such as configuring level ranges or generating labels for arena-type areas. By using this constant, the code maintains consistency and clarity when handling arena areas, simplifying maintenance and reducing potential errors caused by hardcoded values.
AREA_TYPE_ARENA.notes: This constant is stored as a single character, making it efficient for comparison and storage. It is typically used in switch statements or conditional checks to identify arena areas quickly.
AREA_TYPE_ARENA.rationale: The constant exists to provide a clear, maintainable way to identify arena-type areas throughout the codebase, supporting consistent behavior and display logic for such areas.
AREA_TYPE_ARENA.name: AREA_TYPE_ARENA

AREA_TYPE_XXX.brief: A constant character representing the 'XXX' area type identifier used for categorizing specific areas.
AREA_TYPE_XXX.details: The constant `AREA_TYPE_XXX` is a character literal ('X') that uniquely identifies areas of the 'XXX' type within the system. It is initialized with the value 'X' and serves as a standardized marker for this particular area category. This constant is employed in functions such as `scan_credits()` to set the area type and define associated level ranges when the level range string matches 'XXX'. It is also used in switch-case statements to determine display labels and handle logic specific to 'XXX' areas, promoting consistency and clarity in area management across the codebase.
AREA_TYPE_XXX.notes: The constant is stored as a simple character literal, facilitating efficient comparisons and switch-case handling. Its usage pattern emphasizes the importance of consistent identifiers for different area types.
AREA_TYPE_XXX.rationale: This constant exists to provide a clear and maintainable way to identify and handle 'XXX' areas throughout the application, enabling specific behaviors and display logic associated with this area type.
AREA_TYPE_XXX.name: AREA_TYPE_XXX

AREA_TYPE_NORM.brief: The constant `AREA_TYPE_NORM` represents the default 'normal' area type in the application.
AREA_TYPE_NORM.details: `AREA_TYPE_NORM` is a character constant initialized with a space (' ') that signifies a standard or default area classification within the system. It is used throughout the codebase, such as in `Area::scan_credits()`, to denote areas that are considered normal or unclassified. This constant provides a clear and consistent way to identify default area types, facilitating parsing, comparison, and processing of area-related data. Its usage ensures that normal areas are easily distinguished from specialized or other custom area types.
AREA_TYPE_NORM.notes: The constant is stored as a single character, optimizing memory usage and comparison operations. Its value as a space character aligns with common conventions for default or unspecified types.
AREA_TYPE_NORM.rationale: The constant exists to standardize the representation of normal areas across the codebase, simplifying logic related to area classification and ensuring consistent handling of default cases.
AREA_TYPE_NORM.name: AREA_TYPE_NORM

usages.class_area, int Area::scan_credits(): Used as the default area_type when parsing custom level ranges, indicating a normal area.
